name: Madonna
description:
'''Madonna Louise Ciccone'''{{efn|Madonna [[Mononym|goes by her first name]], and has used the name and trademark since 1979 according to the [[World Intellectual Property Organization]].<ref>{{cite news |author=Geneva |date=October 16, 2000 |url=http://edition.cnn.com/2000/TECH/computing/10/16/madonna.cybersquatter.reut/ |title=Madonna wins domain name battle |publisher=[[CNN]] |url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210119165330/http://edition.cnn.com/2000/TECH/computing/10/16/madonna.cybersquatter.reut/ |archive-date=January 19, 2021 |access-date=July 30, 2022}}</ref>}} ({{IPAc-en|tʃ|ᵻ|ˈ|k|oʊ|n|i}} {{respell|chih|KOH|nee}}; born August 16, 1958) is an American singer, songwriter, record producer, and actress. Dubbed the "'''Queen of Pop'''", she is known for her continual reinvention and versatility in music production, songwriting, and visual presentation. Her works incorporate social, political, [[Madonna and sexuality|sexual]], and [[Madonna and religion|religious]] themes, generating both controversy and critical acclaim. Madonna has had [[Cultural impact of Madonna|a significant socio-cultural impact]] across both the 20th and 21st centuries and is regarded as one of the most influential figures in [[popular music]].

Madonna moved to New York City in 1978 to pursue a career in dance. After performing as a drummer, guitarist, and vocalist in the rock bands [[Breakfast Club (band)|Breakfast Club]] and Emmy, she rose to solo stardom with her debut studio album, ''[[Madonna (album)|Madonna]]'' (1983). She has since earned [[Madonna albums discography|eighteen multi-platinum albums]] globally, including ''[[Like a Virgin (album)|Like a Virgin]]'' (1984), ''[[True Blue (Madonna album)|True Blue]]'' (1986), and ''[[The Immaculate Collection]]'' (1990)—some of the [[List of best-selling albums|best-selling albums of all time]]—and ''[[Confessions on a Dance Floor]]'' (2005), her [[List of best-selling albums of the 21st century|21st-century bestseller]]. ''[[Like a Prayer (album)|Like a Prayer]]'' (1989), ''[[Ray of Light]]'' (1998), and ''[[Music (Madonna album)|Music]]'' (2000) were ranked among ''[[Rolling Stone]]''{{'}}s [[Rolling Stone's 500 Greatest Albums of All Time|greatest albums of all time]]. Madonna's top-charting [[Madonna singles discography|singles]] include "[[Like a Virgin (song)|Like a Virgin]]", "[[Papa Don't Preach]]", "[[La Isla Bonita]]", "[[Like a Prayer (song)|Like a Prayer]]", "[[Vogue (Madonna song)|Vogue]]", "[[Take a Bow (Madonna song)|Take a Bow]]", "[[Frozen (Madonna song)|Frozen]]", "[[Music (Madonna song)|Music]]", "[[Hung Up]]", and "[[4 Minutes]]". 

[[Madonna filmography|Madonna has starred in films]] such as ''[[Desperately Seeking Susan]]'' (1985), ''[[Dick Tracy (1990 film)|Dick Tracy]]'' (1990), ''[[A League of Their Own]]'' (1992), and ''[[Evita (1996 film)|Evita]]'' (1996). Although many of her films were not well-received, she won the [[Golden Globe Award for Best Actress – Motion Picture Comedy or Musical|Golden Globe Award for Best Actress]] for playing [[Eva Perón|the titlular politician]] in ''Evita''. [[Madonna and business|Her business endeavors]] encompass the entertainment company [[Maverick (company)|Maverick]] (1992–2009), which included Maverick Records—one of the most successful [[vanity label|artist-run labels]]. Madonna has also pursued [[Madonna fashion brands|fashion brands]], [[Madonna bibliography|written works]], [[Hard Candy Fitness|health clubs]], and filmmaking. She contributes to [[Philanthropy and activism of Madonna|various charities]], having founded the [[Ray of Light Foundation]] in 1998 and [[Raising Malawi]] in 2006, and advocates for [[Feminism of Madonna|gender equality]] and [[Madonna as a gay icon|LGBT rights]].

Madonna is the [[List of best-selling music artists|best-selling female music artist of all time]] and the [[List of highest-grossing live music artists|first female performer]] to accumulate US$1 billion from [[List of Madonna concerts|her concerts]]. She has twelve number-one singles on the US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] and nine number-one albums on the US [[Billboard 200|''Billboard'' 200]]. [[List of awards and nominations received by Madonna|Her accolades]] include seven [[Grammy Awards]], two [[Golden Globe Awards]], twenty [[MTV Video Music Awards]], and an [[List of Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees|induction]] into the [[Rock and Roll Hall of Fame]] in her first year of eligibility. Madonna was the world's [[Forbes list of the world's highest-paid musicians#Female|highest-paid female musician]] for a record eleven years across four decades (1980s–2010s). She has become the subject of various [[List of academic publishing works on Madonna|scholarly]], [[Bibliography of works on Madonna|literary]], and [[Madonna and contemporary arts|artistic]] works, giving rise to an academic subfield known as [[Madonna studies]].
